entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The host plant affected by dry root rot

entities.name: DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting chickpea production

entities.name: RHIZOCTONIA BATATICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes dry root rot

entities.name: LOW SOIL MOISTURE STRESS
entities.description: A climatic factor that increases the incidence of dry root rot

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURE
entities.description: A climatic factor that increases the incidence of dry root rot

entities.name: HOST PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to resist disease, which can be enhanced via ISR

entities.name: INDUCED SYSTEMIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: A mechanism of plant defense mediated by phyto-hormones

entities.name: PHYTO-HORMONES
entities.description: Chemical messengers used to induce systemic resistance in plants

entities.name: METHYL JASMONIC ACID
entities.description: A phyto-hormone used to induce systemic resistance against dry root rot

entities.name: SALICYLIC ACID
entities.description: A phyto-hormone used to induce systemic resistance against dry root rot

entities.name: PER-CENT DISEASE SUSCEPTIBILITY INDEX
entities.description: A metric derived from a modified 0-9 rating scale to measure disease severity

entities.name: REAL-TIME QPCR
entities.description: A technique used for absolute quantification of fungal propagule concentrations
